steps.title: Set up the inequality
steps.content:
Julissa needs at least 100 hours total and currently has 86 hours. If we let $x$ = additional hours needed, then her total will be $86 + x$. The requirement becomes:
$$86 + x \geq 100$$

steps.title: Solve for the minimum
steps.content:
Subtract 86 from both sides to isolate $x$:
$$x \geq 100 - 86$$
$$x \geq 14$$
The minimum number of additional hours is 14. That matches choice A — A is correct.
